skill.name: testing
skill.description: Guides AI assistants through Playwright, Vitest, and Storybook testing workflows for TypeScript and React projects.
skill.summary: Create and debug reliable Playwright, Vitest, and Storybook tests for TypeScript and React apps.
skill.author: CrazySwami

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many backtick, weak-crypto, and reconnaissance patterns, but the reviewed file is a Markdown testing guide with inline code examples. The confirmed residual risk is legitimate Bash usage for running project test scripts, which can execute local package commands and should be disclosed before publication.

medium_findings.title: Bash Test Command Execution Requires Trusted Project Scripts
medium_findings.description: The skill declares Bash access and recommends running pnpm test commands. This is appropriate for a testing skill, but local package scripts can execute arbitrary project-defined commands, so users should run it only in trusted repositories.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Bash is explicitly allowed and the documented commands invoke project package scripts. The context is legitimate testing, but the execution risk depends on repository trust.

low_findings.title: Markdown Inline Code Misclassified as Ruby Shell Backticks
low_findings.description: The external command findings mostly point to Markdown inline code, tables, and fenced TypeScript examples. These are documentation examples and are not Ruby backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown formatting and example snippets, not executable Ruby source. No Ruby file or shell interpolation pattern is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Keyword False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography hits correspond to ordinary testing terms such as description, test.describe, test, vitest, and descriptive. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API usage is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review shows testing framework words, not DES, MD5, SHA1, or crypto implementation code. The skill contains documentation only.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Findings Reflect Legitimate Source Inspection
low_findings.description: The skill asks the assistant to read component source files before writing tests and to avoid guessing selectors. This is normal test authoring behavior and not system reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The source-reading guidance is limited to application components and selector stability. No host fingerprinting, secret discovery, or environment enumeration is requested.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Test Script Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes Bash-based pnpm commands for Playwright test execution. These commands are useful but can trigger arbitrary scripts defined by the local project.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and benign in purpose, but package scripts are controlled by the repository. This creates a contextual execution risk rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

content.value_statement: Teams need tests that match real component behavior and avoid fragile selectors. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Playwright, Vitest, and Storybook test workflows.
actual_capabilities: Guides Playwright E2E test creation for critical user flows.
Recommends stable selectors based on data-testid, role, title, aria-label, and text priority.
Provides Vitest unit and hook testing patterns for TypeScript modules.
Shows Storybook interaction test patterns using canvas queries and user events.
Defines testing checklists for happy paths, edge cases, errors, loading states, and isolation.
limitations: It is a Markdown guidance skill, not an automated test runner.
It assumes a TypeScript, React, Playwright, Vitest, and Storybook stack.
It does not include generated helper files or project-specific fixtures.
Bash test commands should only run in trusted repositories.

use_cases.title: Add E2E Coverage
use_cases.description: Create Playwright tests for editor flows using selectors confirmed from component source files.

use_cases.title: Debug Failing Tests
use_cases.description: Review brittle selectors, async state handling, and helper usage when browser tests fail.

use_cases.title: Standardize Test Patterns
use_cases.description: Align team test files around consistent naming, focused assertions, and reliable interaction patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Write a Basic Unit Test
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner task for a small utility or component function.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the testing skill to write Vitest tests for this module. Cover the normal case and one clear edge case.

prompt_templates.title: Create an E2E Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate task for a user-facing feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the testing skill to inspect the relevant components first, then write a Playwright E2E test for this workflow using stable selectors.

prompt_templates.title: Fix a Brittle Test
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate debugging task for flaky browser tests.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the testing skill to review this failing Playwright test. Replace fragile selectors and explain the likely failure cause.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Coverage Improvements
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced task for a broader test strategy review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the testing skill to audit this feature area. Propose E2E, unit, and Storybook interaction tests with priority and rationale.

output_examples.input: Ask for Playwright coverage for an editor toolbar.
output_examples.output: The assistant reads the toolbar source, identifies title-based selectors, writes focused browser tests, and notes which flows remain uncovered.

output_examples.input: Ask why a Vitest hook test is failing after an async action.
output_examples.output: The assistant checks state updates, recommends wrapping async changes in act, and suggests assertions for initial and updated values.

output_examples.input: Ask for Storybook interaction coverage for a form component.
output_examples.output: The assistant proposes canvas-based role queries, user interactions, and visible result assertions without relying on implementation details.

best_practices: Read component source before choosing selectors for E2E tests.
Prefer user-visible behavior over implementation details in assertions.
Mock external dependencies so tests stay focused and repeatable.
anti_patterns: Do not guess toolbar text or selector names without source evidence.
Do not rely on CSS classes, XPath, or generated IDs for browser tests.
Do not run Bash test commands in untrusted repositories.

faq.question: What testing tools does this skill support?
faq.answer: It focuses on Playwright, Vitest, and Storybook interaction tests for TypeScript and React projects.

faq.question: Can it run tests automatically?
faq.answer: It documents pnpm test commands, but actual execution depends on the assistant tool and user approval model.

faq.question: Why does it require reading source files first?
faq.answer: Source review helps the assistant use stable selectors instead of guessing fragile labels or markup.

faq.question: Is this skill safe for any repository?
faq.answer: Use caution with untrusted repositories because package test scripts can execute local commands.

faq.question: Does it create project-specific fixtures?
faq.answer: No. It describes fixture patterns and helper methods, but the assistant must adapt them to the project.

faq.question: Can it improve existing failing tests?
faq.answer: Yes. It helps review selectors, async behavior, helper usage, and missing assertions.
